explanations.3: The parentheses create a subshell, which is like a separate environment that won't affect your current shell — useful here to keep the password variable isolated.
explanations.4: The && means 'only run the next command if this one succeeds', so the docker command only starts if the password assignment works.
explanations.5: $(pwd) runs the pwd command and inserts its output (your current directory) into the string, and $(id -u):$(id -g) does the same to get your user and group IDs so the container runs as you instead of root.
